item.title: Vance did not travel to Switzerland Thursday night for U.S.-Iran talks, White House says
item.description: "We look forward to beginning technical talks as soon as possible," a White House spokesperson said Thursday night.

item.title: What's the HELOC and home equity loan interest rate forecast for summer 2026?
item.description: HELOC and home equity rates have been on the move lately. Here's where those rates could head next.

item.title: When could homeowners realistically expect to refinance? 4 lending experts weigh in
item.description: The right time to refinance depends on a few factors, experts say, including your current mortgage loan rate.

item.title: Cuba approves free-market reforms in effort to stave off economic collapse
item.description: Cuban lawmakers Thursday adopted nearly 200 historic free-market reforms aimed at rescuing the communist island from a severe crisis aggravated by a U.S. oil blockade.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: 2023 video shows Mike Rogers seeming to lunge at Matt Gaetz on House floor
item.description: Video shows the moment Republican Rep. Mike Rogers of Alabama was pulled away forcefully on the Congress floor after moving toward his fellow Republican Rep. Matt Gaetz on Jan 6, 2023.

item.title: Researchers find "Pink Planet" has salty clouds: "We were very surprised"
item.description: The "Pink Planet," formally known as GJ504b, was discovered in 2013 and is technically not a planet but rather a "planetary-mass companion."

item.title: Latest U.S. strike on alleged drug boat kills 3 in eastern Pacific, Pentagon says
item.description: The U.S. military has attacked a boat accused of smuggling drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean, killing three people.

item.title: Tornadoes tear through multiple states across Midwest, Southeast
item.description: Tornadoes were reported in Illinois, Iowa and several other states Wednesday as severe weather slammed a large swath of the Midwest​ and Southeast.

item.title: Remnants of Tropical Storm Arthur flood homes and force evacuations in Gulf states
item.description: The remnants of Tropical Storm Arthur were battering parts of the southeastern U.S. with heavy rain, sparking flash flooding.

item.title: Comparing Trump and Obama's Iran deals
item.description: The Trump administration has said repeatedly that the memorandum of understanding between Iran and the U.S. is better than the deal negotiated by the Obama administration in 2015. "The Daily Report" breaks down both deals.

item.title: Why Democratic Socialism movement is growing in major U.S. cities
item.description: CBS News projects Democratic socialist Janeese Lewis George will win the Washington, D.C., mayoral primary. CBS News' Fin Gómez has more.

item.title: Trump's goals for the Iran war and what he's saying now
item.description: When the U.S.-Iran conflict began, President Trump laid out a litany of aggressive war aims. Here's what the president and his top aides said then — and how their views have changed.

item.title: Comedian Carlos Mencia charged with tax fraud in LA
item.description: Carlos Mencia is accused of failing to report $8.7 million in income over a six-year period.

item.title: Luigi Mangione's legal team withdraws psychiatric defense
item.description: Luigi Mangione's legal team says they withdrew a psychiatric defense​​ in his New York state murder trial one day after telling the court they would use it.

item.title: 6/18: CBS Evening News
item.description: Life-threatening flash floods hit the South; White House hits critics over the Iran deal.

item.title: States not required to give community-based care for those with disabilities: DOJ opinion
item.description: The Office of Legal Counsel opinion released Thursday said states aren't required by law to integrate mentally disabled patients with their peers by providing community or home-based care.

item.title: America's long history of political turmoil
item.description: In his new book, "Been There, Done That," historian Greg Jackson shines a light on dark times in U.S. history, such as insurrections, political partisanship and disregard for the Constitution. Jackson joins "The Takeout" with more.

item.title: Details after Trump signs peace deal with Iran
item.description: The clock started ticking for the U.S. and Iran to reach a final long-term peace deal after the presidents of both countries signed off on the 14-point memorandum of understanding on Wednesday. CBS News White House reporter Aaron Navarro has more.

item.title: Rep. Espaillat brushes off primary challenge: "I've been in this neighborhood for a long time"
item.description: Democratic Rep. Adriano Espaillat, who chairs the Congressional Hispanic Caucus, has been one of the fiercest critics of President Trump, dating back to the president's first term. But the New Yorker now faces a primary challenger from his left who believes he hasn't been fierce enough and also has the backing of New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani. Espaillat joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: Air Force unit faces flu outbreak weeks after vaccine mandate dropped
item.description: Dozens of service members at Lackland Air Force Base in Texas have fallen ill with the flu in the weeks since Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth rescinded the vaccine mandate, sources familiar confirmed to CBS News.

item.title: How the Obama Presidential Center is unique among presidential libraries
item.description: Former presidents and world leaders gathered in Chicago on Thursday to celebrate the grand opening of the Obama Presidential Center. CBS News congressional correspondent Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

item.title: H. R. McMaster says preliminary Iran deal "cuts very significantly against U.S. interests"
item.description: President Trump's memorandum of understanding with Iran is being met with criticism from both Republicans and Democrats. H. R. McMaster, former national security adviser to Mr. Trump, joins with his perspective on the deal.

item.title: Trump administration plans aggressive expansion of denaturalization cases
item.description: A Justice Department official tells CBS News that the Trump administration plans to revoke the citizenship of more than 250 foreign-born citizens by the fall. CBS News immigration correspondent Camilo Montoya-Galvez has the details.

item.title: Netanyahu says Israel won't withdraw from Lebanon, flouting U.S.-Iran deal
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday said the Israeli military will not leave its security zone in southern Lebanon despite the Iran memorandum of understanding. CBS News senior White House correspondent Weijia Jiang has the latest.

item.title: Former first lady Michelle Obama praises her husband in presidential center dedication speech
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama spoke at the dedication ceremony of the Obama Presidential Center in Chicago on Thursday, calling it "an honor" to be by her husband's side. Watch her full speech.

item.title: Watch: Obama speaks at dedication of presidential center in Chicago
item.description: Former President Barack Obama's new presidential center in Chicago held its grand opening on Thursday. Speaking at the dedication ceremony, Obama said he hopes the center will affirm "how precious our democracy really is."

item.title: Americans say they're reading fewer books. Here's what they're doing instead.
item.description: Many people are spending more time on screens, but also doing more physical activities, a new CBS News poll finds.

item.title: U.S. plans expansion of denaturalization push, aiming for 250 cases by fall
item.description: The Trump administration expects to try to revoke the U.S. citizenship of more than 250 foreign-born citizens by October, a Justice Department official said.

item.title: Explore the Obama Presidential Center and Library opening in Chicago
item.description: After more than a decade since it was announced, the Obama Presidential Center and Library officially opens to the public in Chicago's Jackson Park on June 19, 2026.
